contribution.title: Schwarzschild solution to Einstein's field equations
contribution.description: First exact non-trivial solution to Einstein's vacuum field equations. Describes the spacetime outside a non-rotating, uncharged, spherically symmetric mass.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Foundational. The Schwarzschild metric is the analytic baseline of this project.

contribution.title: Schwarzschild radius
contribution.description: Defined the characteristic radius r_s = 2GM/c^2 at which the metric becomes singular in Schwarzschild coordinates (the event horizon for non-rotating black holes).
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Implemented in src/BlackHoleDS.cpp and tested in tests/smoke_tests.cpp.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Find exact closed-form solutions when possible; verify by substitution into the original equations.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the simplest geometry that still captures the essential physics?
Where does this solution break down, and what is happening there physically vs mathematically?
Can I separate variables exploiting the symmetry?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Mistaking a coordinate singularity for a physical singularity (r = r_s vs r = 0).

reference.citation: Schwarzschild, K. (1916). Uber das Gravitationsfeld eines Massenpunktes nach der Einsteinschen Theorie. Sitzungsberichte der Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Designing or validating any module that touches non-rotating, vacuum-spacetime geometry.
how_to_apply.cautions: His original coordinate system has a coordinate singularity at r = r_s. Use Eddington-Finkelstein or Kruskal-Szekeres coordinates near the horizon.
